candidates.stmt: I've represented Berechurch residents on Colchester City Council since 2016. I would like to continue doing so.  Alongside my colleagues Chris Pearson, Dave Harris and Pam  Cox, I bring a wealth of experience to the table to help get things done and represent the interests of local residents.  Together we work all year round and not just at election times.  We lead community litter-picks to help keep Berechurch tidy and regularly knock on residents doors to ask them if they have local issues we can help them with.  People tell us that we 'are the only ones they ever see'.  So, please vote for me on 7th May and keep backing your Berechurch Councillors.

I was born in Colchester. I grew up here & attended The Gilberd School. I’ve lived in Highwoods for twenty years.
I’ve started & run several Highwoods businesses & I’ve raised my family here.

Residents of Constable Division are united by their love of where they live whether it's in the rural villages, as I do, or north Highwoods on the city's north-easterly boundary.

This division of Essex County Council takes in the northerly villages of Lexden and Braiswick ward of Colchester City Council, which I've represented since 2022 and where I'm seeking re-election too. I want to continue to fight for you at Colchester.

I've also decided to stand for election for the first time to Essex County Council because the experience and knowledge I've gained at Colchester council will be vital to making sure our voice is heard loud and clear at County Hall in Chelmsford.

I am principled as well as determined. I successfully campaigned as a councillor for local elections to go ahead on 7 May 2026. These local elections are more important than ever because major decisions will be taken in the next year at Essex and Colchester councils that change our communities for ever.

Now is not the time to try out unknown or new candidates.

Vote Sara Naylor to be your champion for this most north-easterly part of Essex where our communities and way of life is being disrespected by, and, is under threat from Labour's plans:
- to build needlessly over our green fields and erect mega-pylons, and 
- to deny us the better roads, extra hospital beds, school places, GPs and sewage /water capacity that we've proved are desperately needed now.

This election is between the Green Party and the Reform Party. It is a battle between hope and hate. Vote for Green Party Candidates in the local election 7 May 2026.

The Greens won Castle Ward, Colchester in recent years: Amy 2022 with 1420 (50.2%) of 2820 votes, Mark 2023 with 1374 (50.4%) of 2727 votes, Kemal in 2024 with 1247 (44.5%) of 2805 votes. The locals of Castle Ward benefit from the three Green Party councillors. This can be done in Marks Tey & Layer and other Wards as well. You deserve better. You deserve hard working Green Party councillors as Berthold.

In East of England the former leader Adrian Ramsay was elected MP for Waveney Valley with 20,467 votes (41.7%) and a majority of 5,593 over the second-placed Conservative candidate and this year Hannah Spencer is the first Green Party member to win a parliamentary by-election, and the party's first MP in the north of England. She won the by-election with 40.7% of the votes, a majority of 4,402. Reform second and Labour third. Conservatives and LibDem lost their deposits.

Berthold joint the Green Party of England and Wales in 1999 when he was working for Imperial College in London. In January 2009 he moved to Colchester when he joint the University of Essex. He was Professor of Data Science and Statistics. August 2025 he retired from the University of Essex and continues to serve as Emeritus Professor.

You find our Local Manifesto (Updated 2023) under https://colchestergreenparty.co.uk/our-local-manifesto/

David’s is a former Community Councillor and Colchester Chair of Governors. He lives in Mile End, with family nearby and loves the area.  He served in the Senior Civil Service and wants to bring those skills and experiences to help others. With Martin Goss, Phil Coleman and Anne Turrell he has been helping the local Lib Dem team to ‘make a difference’, to respond to community needs as they arise and to lend a hand.  His priorities include arguing for ban on non-recyclable plastics, zero tolerance on dog fouling, litter and anti-social behaviour and action on the issues that matter most to the people of Mile End. David is the Cabinet lead on business and resources, and has also taken the lead for the Council on tree planting across the borough, the Colchester Orbital and action to reduce non-recyclable plastics.

Hello, I’m Kayleigh, a mum, community organiser, and your local councillor.

This is my home. It’s where I’m raising my family, and where I’ve chosen to put my energy, working alongside people to make things better.

I believe the best change happens when we co create it together. Not decisions made at a distance, but people coming together, sharing ideas, and building something that truly reflects the community.

Through my work supporting families, I see every day both the pressures people are under and the strength that exists here. When that energy is brought together, it can create real, lasting change.

That’s what drives me. Bringing people together, working with the voluntary sector, securing funding for our community spaces, and turning ideas like the café on the Rec into something real. Everything I do is about creating opportunities with the community, not just for it.

I’m standing again because we’ve started something here, and there’s so much more we can build together, especially for our young people and the future of our community.

If you see me out and about, come and have a chat.

Let’s keep building it together. Let’s create change

Dear Resident

Let me take this opportunity to introduce myself and a little bit about why I am so passionate about representing the interests of Prettygate, where I work.

Having spent five years working alongside Cllr Sue Lissimore, Cllr Roger Buston and Cllr Leigh Tate across Prettgate and Lexden - now is the time to step and be its next city councillor.

With over twenty years experience working in Healthcare here in North Essex and specifically Colchester I have held management, clinical and voluntary positions with most of our NHS hospitals, private hospital providers, healthcare charities and local Ambulance service.

In 2012 I established a not-for-profit healthcare organisation providing free or affordable event medical provision and first aid training to hundreds of public, community and sporting events and charitable organisations across the East of England region and wider UK. Growing year on year to become the largest not-for-profit ambulance provider in East Anglia. The most rewarding highlight for me has been in supporting, mentoring and training multiple local young people to fulfill their dreams of becoming registered Nurses, Paramedics and other Allied Healthcare Professionals. I continued this passion for education as a part time Clinical Lecturer for Anaesthetic, paramedical and nursing students at a local university.

In 2023 I decided to swap nights shifts for more time with my son and young family. Since then I have been a postman and currently a post office worker here in The Commons, Prettygate.

I am married to Angela, a Ministry of Defence public servant of 28 years from Colchester and with a 9 year old son, community safety, cleanliness and improving the assessability of public services, recreational facilities and educational access Prettygate are also issues of pressing importance and very very close to my own heart.

After 5 years being proactive in Prettygate (lobbying, litterpicking, grafetti removal, sign cleaning and painting), I n

I am honoured to be standing as the Reform UK candidate for Rochford North in the upcoming Essex County Council elections. I am committed to being a strong local voice and fighting for the issues that matter most to local residents — especially protecting our precious green spaces from overdevelopment.
About Me
I live with my 19-year-old son, our three much-loved cats, and our beloved dog, Marley. As a single parent and small business owner, I understand the pressures many local families are facing and how important it is to preserve the character, community spirit, and environment of the places we call home.
Professional Background
I am the proud founder and owner of a successful hospitality and catering recruitment agency. Over 22 years ago, I started my business with just £300 and no previous business experience. Through determination, resilience, and hard work, I built it into a respected and successful local company.
Living with severe dyslexia has taught me perseverance and the importance of never giving up. I am proud that my business has received recognition for excellent customer service and for supporting local people into work.
Protecting Rochford North’s Green Spaces
One of the biggest reasons I decided to stand is because I care deeply about protecting our green spaces, countryside, and the identity of our local communities.
Residents across Rochford North are deeply concerned about overdevelopment, the loss of greenbelt land, increasing pressure on local infrastructure, traffic congestion, and the strain being placed on local services. I share those concerns wholeheartedly.
Once our green spaces are gone, they are gone forever.
I believe we must stand up against inappropriate development and fight to preserve the fields, open land, wildlife, and natural environment that make our area such a wonderful place to live. Future generations deserve to grow up enjoying the same beautiful surroundings that many of us have cherished for years.
If elected, I will

John has lived and been involved in the Prettygate and Lexden area  since he first moved to Colchester as a child. Now retired, John worked in the NHS and in local government as and HR manager. When a member of Essex County Council, John was instrumental in making sure that Lexden Park was not turned into another housing estate and that County handed it over to Colchester Borough Council as a public open space. John has actively campaigned for residents of Prettygate over the last 10 years

Campaigning for financial common sense and infrastructure first at Colchester City Council. Standing for re-election on 🗳️𝟳 𝗠𝗮𝘆 𝟮𝟬𝟮𝟲 (Gt Tey, Chappel, Wakes Colne, Mount Bures, Fordham, Wormingford, Gt Horkesley, Little Horkesley, Boxted, Langham, Dedham)
